events.change_summary: Intent check: globe viz + PyPI packaging scope

events.summary: Intent check pushed: globe 3D viz via --globe flag (geo.py + globe.gl HTML) plus PyPI packaging metadata and LICENSE file

events.change_summary: Full scope: globe viz + PyPI packaging — 12 requirements, 10 ACs, 2 phases

events.rationale: New Python features (globe module + packaging metadata) with no UI components to design. Skip design: the globe's color scheme is derived from existing display.py thresholds; Globe.gl handles all rendering decisions. Skip architecture: the approach is fully specified in the scope (ip-api.com batch + Globe.gl CDN HTML + tempfile/webbrowser stdlib); only 3 files touched plus 3 new files, well within a single-phase implementation review.

events.decision: Use ip-api.com batch API for hop geolocation
events.rationale: Free, no API key required, supports up to 100 IPs per batch call, returns lat/lon in a single HTTP request. The free tier rate limit (45 req/min) is not a concern for typical probe sizes (≤50 hops). Alternatives considered: MaxMind GeoLite2 (requires bundling a large binary database and a reader dependency), ipinfo.io (free tier requires registration). ip-api.com is the lowest-friction path and keeps the install footprint minimal.

events.decision: Use Globe.gl via CDN for 3D globe rendering
events.rationale: Globe.gl provides a declarative arc/point/label API on a Three.js sphere with no bundler required. Loading from unpkg CDN keeps the generated HTML self-contained as a single file with no Python build-time dependencies. Alternatives: D3.js + topojson (more code to write, less visual impact out of the box); Cesium (heavyweight, requires API key); pure Three.js (would need to write arc math from scratch).

events.decision: Extract latency threshold constants from display.py rather than duplicating them in globe.py
events.rationale: The per-hop thresholds (green <20 ms, yellow 20–79 ms, red ≥80 ms) currently live inline inside fmt_latency() in display.py. Globe.py needs the same values for arc coloring. Extracting them as module-level constants (LATENCY_GREEN_MS, LATENCY_YELLOW_MS) ensures one source of truth and prevents drift if the thresholds are ever adjusted.

events.summary: Full scope written as revision 2. 12 requirements across two feature areas, 10 acceptance criteria, 2 implementation phases (Phase 1: PyPI packaging — Small effort; Phase 2: Globe visualization — Medium effort). Key decisions logged: ip-api.com batch API for geolocation, Globe.gl via CDN for rendering, shared latency threshold constants from display.py. Workflow: backend-feature, standard tier, scope → implementation → review → release.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting Phase 1: PyPI packaging — creating README.md, LICENSE, and updating pyproject.toml

events.description: Implementing core changes: README.md, LICENSE, pyproject.toml metadata

events.details: Build verification: python -m build produced netpath-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl and netpath-0.1.0.tar.gz with no warnings. pip install dist/*.whl succeeded. netpath --help shows correct version. pip show netpath / METADATA includes author (Nick Moore), Homepage URL (https://github.com/holynakamoto/netpath), Bug Tracker URL, all 9 classifiers, LICENSE-File reference, and README description content. No project-level test suite exists in this repo.

events.decision: Implementation: used Development Status :: 4 - Beta classifier for 0.1.0 release
events.rationale: The package is pre-1.0 and usable but not feature-complete, which matches Beta status. Alpha would imply early prototype; RC would overstate readiness. Beta is the PyPI convention for a functional 0.x release.

events.summary: Created README.md (install instructions, usage, prerequisites, CF token docs), LICENSE (MIT 2026), and extended pyproject.toml with authors, readme pointer, project URLs, and classifiers. Build verified: wheel and sdist produce clean output; pip install + netpath --help succeed; METADATA contains all required PyPI quality fields.

items.description: In a clean venv, run `python -m build` from the repo root and confirm it prints 'Successfully built netpath-0.1.0.tar.gz and netpath-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl' with no warnings.
Run `pip install dist/netpath-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl` in the clean venv, then run `netpath --help` and confirm it exits 0 and shows the `asn` and `country` subcommands.
Run `pip show netpath` and confirm the output includes `Author-email: Nick Moore <nickm@dave.io>` and `Project-URL: Homepage, https://github.com/holynakamoto/netpath`.
Open `README.md` in a GitHub preview or markdown renderer and confirm no broken formatting — all code blocks use triple-backtick fences and render correctly.
Open `LICENSE` and confirm it contains MIT license text with year `2026` and author `Nick Moore`.

Inspect `pyproject.toml` and confirm `readme = "README.md"`, an `authors` entry, a `[project.urls]` table with `Homepage` and `Bug Tracker`, and classifiers listing Python 3.9 through 3.13 are all present.

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: PyPI Packaging

### Files Changed
- `README.md` (new) — project description, pip/uvx/uv install instructions, mtr and iperf3 prerequisites, usage examples for both subcommands, NETPATH_CF_TOKEN note, Cloudflare dashboard link
- `LICENSE` (new) — MIT license text, year 2026, author Nick Moore
- `pyproject.toml` (modified) — added `readme = "README.md"`, `authors` list, `[project.urls]` table (Homepage + Bug Tracker), and 9 classifiers (Development Status Beta, MIT License, OS Independent, Python 3.9–3.13, Networking Monitoring)

### Verification Results
- `python -m build` produced `netpath-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl` and `netpath-0.1.0.tar.gz` with no warnings
- `pip install dist/*.whl && netpath --help` exited 0 showing correct version and subcommands
- `pip show netpath` METADATA includes Author-email (Nick Moore), Project-URL Homepage, Project-URL Bug Tracker, License-File: LICENSE, all classifiers, and README description
- `dist/` directory created as build verification artifact — not part of the commit

### Deviations from Plan
None. hatchling automatically picked up the `LICENSE` file and emitted a `License-File: LICENSE` entry in METADATA without any extra configuration.
